objective.problem: Decide whether individually weak IdrEvent signals form a credible campaign when their order and infrastructure relationships are evaluated together.
objective.decision_target: Advisory IntelligenceFinding (campaign probability + ranked entities + evidence event IDs) consumed by idr-sentinel deterministic corroboration.
success_metrics: Held-out ROC-AUC / PR-AUC / Brier under chronological 60/20/20 splits
S6+GNN hybrid must beat or match every ablation variant on Brier under the same split
Every finding carries evidence_event_ids resolvable to source telemetry

constraints: Advisory boundary: model output must never trigger PanicResponse; idr-sentinel corroborates against primary telemetry first
Latent state is never an auditable substitute for source events; evidence IDs are mandatory in findings
Chronological splits only; no training on future information relative to prediction time
Synthetic benchmark results must never be presented as production detection accuracy
python >= 3.11; deps: numpy, torch >= 2.2, scikit-learn; GPL-3.0-or-later

sources: NDJSON streams of idr_common::IdrEvent from idr-main; synthetic matched campaigns from simulator.py
schemas: IdrEvent: {id, timestamp, source, severity, kind{type,...}, metadata} — validated in schema.py
provenance: Simulator seeds are deterministic; event IDs are seed-derived UUIDs

outputs: IntelligenceFinding JSON (pipeline.py): campaign_id, escalation_probability, predicted_next_stage (rule-based), related_entities, evidence_event_ids, model_version, graph_nodes, graph_relations

components.role: entity extraction, typed edges, 20-dim feature vector
temporal graph: per-entity histories, normalized adjacency, evidence provenance

interfaces.name: IdrEvent
interfaces.contract: canonical tagged shape from idr_common; kind must contain 'type'

interfaces.name: checkpoint
interfaces.contract: dict {state_dict, feature_dim, hidden_dim, state_dim, use_s6, use_gnn, pooling}; v2 dicts lacking flags default to hybrid with pooling inferred from keys; v1 raw state dicts infer everything from tensor shapes/keys and load as pooling=uniform

interfaces.name: TemporalGraph
interfaces.contract: node_ids, sequences [N,steps,20], mask, symmetric degree-normalized adjacency, evidence_ids per node, relation_counts

interfaces.name: IntelligenceFinding
interfaces.contract: see outputs; predicted_next_stage is a heuristic lookup, not learned

decisions.decision: Advisory-only integration; findings feed idr-sentinel, never actuate responses
Explicit per-step S6 scan chosen for auditability over throughput in v0.1
Masked max-pools fill with -inf so isfinite guards genuinely zero all-padding rows (was -1e9 no-op)
Ablation variants construct only the submodules they use, keeping parameter counts honest
Checkpoints carry their own dimensions; CLI must not hardcode hyperparameters
campaign_id derives from chronologically first event, independent of caller ordering
Push CI restricted to main; PR commits build once via pull_request trigger
mypy follows the running interpreter version; pinning 3.11 breaks against numpy 2.5 PEP 695 stubs on newer Pythons

Gated MIL attention pooling replaces mean+max in the default model: attention scores are the entity ranking, so evidence selection finally receives training gradient (node_head previously had grad=None). uniform_pool retained as fifth ablation arm; uniform path stays numerically identical to legacy checkpoints

Design roadmap adopted: 4 phases / 14 workstreams synthesized from 24 judged proposals; docs/ROADMAP.md is canonical. Rejected: learned next-stage head (circular supervision), dense R-GCN typed adjacency (unfalsifiable pre-Phase-2)
Checkpoints persist ablation flags and pooling mode so every trained variant round-trips (verify-workflow finding); non-dict payloads rejected with a clear error

completed: v0.1.0 package merged to main (PR #2); CI lint fix (PR #5); Ocean Reef takeoff migrated out to LeeLupton/pricing (PR #4, pricing#1)
Review follow-ups + test hardening (4->14) + docstrings + mypy gate (PR #6)
Design roadmap synthesized and committed (docs/ROADMAP.md); state task graph now mirrors it

Adversarial diff verification of PR #6 (19 agents): 4 distinct upheld findings, all fixed in the W1 slice (variant round-trip, legacy-shape test, vacuous order-invariance test, roundtrip weight fidelity)
W1: attention-gated pooling + evidence_precision_at_5 metric + fifth ablation arm
active: W2: EngineConfig + ModelManifest (config.py, registry.py, configs/default.toml; schema-hash rejection on load)
blocked: Phase 1 remainder: W3 calibration+ECE (after W2 manifest), W4 operating-point eval, W5 ATT&CK mapping
Phase 2 (gates phases 3-4): W6 graded/adversarial simulator, W7 benchmark suite+drift, W8 LabeledWindow ingestion, W9 rolling-origin CV, W10 identity entities
Phase 3: W11 per-entity time deltas, W12 bounded decayed sparse graph, W13 S6 step() cell, W14 structured evidence+suppression
Phase 4: W15 streaming scorer, W16 campaign identity registry (independent, can start post-Phase-1), W17 ONNX+Rust bridge

commands_run.result: None on pre-W1 model — confirmed untrained ranking defect

tests: tests/test_system.py + tests/test_state_record.py: 21 tests — adds attention-gradient proof, all-variant checkpoint round-trip with output equality, true legacy-shape load (injected static.* keys), non-vacuous campaign_id order-invariance (distinct id prefixes), roundtrip weight fidelity

benchmarks.roc_pr: all five variants saturate at 1.0 (synthetic separability; Phase 2 W6 exists to fix this)
benchmarks.note: ep@5 chance floor ~0.5 on 9-node graphs; 3 epochs is deliberately minimal — metric exists to make Phase 2 improvements measurable

assumptions: Simulator class separability makes ranking metrics uninformative at n=80; only calibration (Brier) separates variants — W6 is the fix
predicted_next_stage is a 4-rule lookup presented alongside model output; W5 replaces it with an ATT&CK table, still deterministic
evidence_precision_at_5 uses multi-event entity membership as ground truth; valid for current simulator convergence design only
uncertainty: No real labeled campaigns yet (W8); production accuracy unknown
Homogeneous message passing; typed variant deliberately deferred until split-host evasion scenario can falsify it (see roadmap rejection)
Adjacency accumulates over the whole window, no decay (W12); delta_seconds is global, not per-entity (W11)

artifacts.role: canonical 4-phase/14-workstream improvement roadmap with judge scores and rejections
Arch container CI: install, import check, pytest, ruff, mypy

latest_event.summary: Roadmap adopted; W1 attention pooling shipped with verify-workflow fixes folded in; W2 (EngineConfig+Manifest) is next
